concept_name: Magnetic Field of a Bar Magnet
locked_facts: Field lines emerge from N pole, enter S pole OUTSIDE magnet
Inside magnet: field lines go from S to N (continuous closed loops)
truth_statements: Magnetic field lines of a bar magnet form continuous closed loops — they go from N to S outside and from S to N inside the magnet
The field is strongest near the poles where the lines are most concentrated
The axial field is twice the equatorial field at the same distance from the center
A bar magnet is equivalent to a magnetic dipole with moment M = m × 2l
Magnetic monopoles have never been observed — every magnet has both a north and a south pole

aha_moment.what_to_show: Rotate the 3D view to look inside the magnet. Field lines that went N→S outside now continue S→N inside, forming complete closed loops. There are no sources or sinks — unlike electric field lines. This is why monopoles do not exist.
aha_moment.visual_emphasis: Trace a single field line: outside from N to S, then inside from S to N, forming one complete loop. No beginning, no end.
aha_moment.why_this_breaks_misconception: Students see that field lines do not start at N and end at S — they are continuous closed loops

simulation_states.label: Bar magnet — N and S poles
simulation_states.description: A bar magnet with red N-pole and blue S-pole. No field lines yet. Labels identify the poles.
simulation_states.emphasis: A bar magnet with two poles: North (red) and South (blue)

simulation_states.label: External field lines — N to S
simulation_states.description: Field lines appear outside the magnet, curving from N pole to S pole. Arrows show direction. Lines are denser near poles (stronger field).
simulation_states.emphasis: Outside: lines emerge from N and curve back into S. The field is strongest near the poles where lines are densest.

simulation_states.label: Inside the magnet — S to N (closed loops!)
simulation_states.description: Camera rotates to show the interior. Inside the magnet, field lines continue from S to N. Each line forms a complete closed loop. Caption: No monopoles — lines never end!
simulation_states.emphasis: INSIDE the magnet, lines go from S to N — completing the loop! Every field line is a closed curve. There are NO magnetic charges (monopoles).

simulation_states.label: Explore — trace complete loops
simulation_states.description: Interactive mode. Full 3D rotation. Student can trace any field line and verify it forms a closed loop: N→S outside, S→N inside.
simulation_states.emphasis: Trace any single field line: N → S outside → S → N inside → back to start. Closed loop. B_axial = 2 × B_equatorial.

trigger_keywords: N pole se shuru hota hai
common_misconceptions.student_belief: Magnetic field lines start at the north pole and end at the south pole
common_misconceptions.actual_physics: Magnetic field lines are CLOSED LOOPS. Outside the magnet they go from N to S, but inside they continue from S to N. They have no beginning or end. This is because magnetic monopoles do not exist — div B = 0.
common_misconceptions.simulation_emphasis: Show the complete closed loop by rotating to see inside the magnet — lines continue from S to N inside.

common_misconceptions.student_belief: You can isolate a magnetic monopole by cutting a magnet in half
common_misconceptions.actual_physics: If you cut a bar magnet in half, you get two complete bar magnets, each with its own N and S pole. You can never isolate a single pole. This is a fundamental property of magnetism — magnetic monopoles have never been observed.
common_misconceptions.simulation_emphasis: Show the continuous closed-loop nature of field lines — there is no point where lines start or end, so there is no monopole.

typical_question_types: B at axial/equatorial point of bar magnet
torque on bar magnet in external field
comparison of axial and equatorial fields
common_traps: confusing axial and equatorial formulas (factor of 2)
forgetting d is from center not from pole
wrong direction of field at equatorial point
